name: Navigation & Breadcrumbs
description: How category navigation and breadcrumbs should orient users within the catalogue hierarchy and support exploration

heuristics.principle: Breadcrumbs should clearly show the user's position within the category hierarchy and allow easy navigation upward
checkpoints: Breadcrumb trail is visible near the top of the page
Each breadcrumb level is a clickable link to its parent category
Current category is shown as the final non-clickable breadcrumb
Breadcrumbs do not wrap excessively or become truncated

heuristics.principle: Subcategory navigation should be visible to help users refine their browsing without relying solely on filters
checkpoints: Subcategories are displayed as links or visual navigation
Subcategory labels clearly describe the product subset
Current category is distinguished from sibling categories
Subcategory navigation does not duplicate filter functionality unnecessarily

heuristics.principle: The category page title and description should set clear expectations about the product range
checkpoints: Category heading matches the navigation label that was clicked
Category description provides relevant context without excessive length
SEO text does not push product results below the fold
Product count is visible to set expectations about the range
